title: Vegan Breakfast Cookies
image_alt: Vegan breakfast cookies on a cooling rack
modifications: Used soy-free chocolate chunks in place of the dried fruit mix-in option

items.name: soy-free chocolate chunks ✱ replaces dried fruit
pepitas or sunflower seeds (or chopped nuts)

steps: Make the flax egg: combine ground flaxseed meal with 5 Tbsp water. Stir and let sit for 10–15 minutes until gelled.
Arrange racks in the top and bottom thirds of the oven. Preheat to 350°F (175°C). Line two large sheet pans with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, mix together the dry ingredients: oats, coconut, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon if using.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the wet ingredients: almond butter, maple syrup, vanilla, and the prepared flax egg. Whisk until smooth and thick.

Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and mix with a spatula until the dough comes together. Fold in the chocolate chunks, seeds, and hemp seeds. If the dough is too sticky, refrigerate for 10–15 minutes to firm up.
Scoop 35–40g of dough (about 2–2½ Tbsp) per cookie onto the prepared pans, spacing about ½ inch apart — 12 per pan. They don't spread much.
Lightly flatten the top of each cookie with your hand. Bake for 18–20 minutes until the bottoms are lightly golden.
Rest on the pan for 5 minutes, then transfer to a cooling rack. Store in an airtight container on the counter for 5–7 days, or freeze for up to 3 months.
